rationale: The citing paper’s core mechanism tests rely on eye-gaze attention and utility/valuation dynamics in choice (including attentional DDM variants), and the cited paper specifically models eye-movement attention trajectories as utility accumulation to predict choice. That conceptual and methodological overlap (attention-to-utility mechanism with eye-tracking) makes the citation plausibly substantive rather than incidental. Even though the matched evidence is only a DOI-level reference entry, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
